results.question: What are the prerequisites for Project I?
results.answerPreview: Project I requires completed 60 credit hours.

results.question: What is Project I?
results.answerPreview: Project I requires completed 60 credit hours.

results.question: What are the prerequisites for Project II?
results.answerPreview: Project II requires completed 60 credit hours and Project I.

results.question: Tell me about ARP6110-P1.
results.answerPreview: ARP6110-P1 Project I requires completed 60 credit hours.

results.question: What should I know about progression rules?
results.answerPreview: Use the programme structure and prerequisite rules for progression. Project I requires completed 60 credit hours. Project II requires Project I and completed 60 credit hours.

results.question: What courses are listed in Year 1 for Intelligent Robotics?
results.answerPreview: Year 1 for Intelligent Robotics includes Technical Calculus, Computer and Programming, Micro-Controllers & Microprocessors, Electrical Circuits, Basic Electronics, Differential Equations, Digital Design, Linear Algebra and Numerical Methods

results.question: What course codes are listed in Year 1 for Intelligent Robotics?
results.answerPreview: Year 1 for Intelligent Robotics includes ARM6113 Technical Calculus, ARC6113 Computer and Programming, ARC6123 Micro-Controllers & Microprocessors, ARL6113 Electrical Circuits, ARE6113 Basic Electronics, ARM6123 Differential Equations, ARE6

results.question: Which faculty offers the Intelligent Robotics programme?
results.answerPreview: The Intelligent Robotics programme is offered by Faculty of Artificial Intelligence and Engineering (FAIE).

results.question: What are the registration and accreditation identifiers for Intelligent Robotics?
results.answerPreview: The programme page lists registration (R/0788/6/00177) 01/31 and accreditation (MQA/SWA14238).

results.question: What action links are provided on the MMU Intelligent Robotics page?

results.question: What note applies to DKM, DLKM or DVM applicants for Intelligent Robotics?
results.answerPreview: DKM / DLKM / DVM candidates may be required to undergo a Bridging Programme as an additional requirement.

results.question: List the Year 2 Intelligent Robotics courses.
results.answerPreview: Year 2 for Intelligent Robotics includes Linear Systems & Signals, Electromagnetics with Applications, Electrical Machines and Power Systems, Robotics - Machine Design and Mechanisms, Introduction to Artificial Intelligence, Actuators and S

results.question: What courses and activities are in Year 3 for Intelligent Robotics?
results.answerPreview: Year 3 for Intelligent Robotics includes Mobile Robots and Drones, Project I, Project II, Industrial Training, Making Embedded Systems, Robot Programming, Elective 1 BYOC, Elective 2 BYOC, Elective 3 BYOC.

results.question: What is the prerequisite for industrial training in Intelligent Robotics?
results.answerPreview: Industrial Training requires completed 60 credit hours.

results.question: What BYOC elective subjects are offered in March/April for Intelligent Robotics?

results.question: What MPU or university courses are included in Intelligent Robotics?

results.question: How is ARM6113 Technical Calculus assessed?
results.answerPreview: Tests/Quizzes: 40%; Final Examination: 60%.

results.question: What is CLO 1 for ARM6113?
results.answerPreview: CLO 1 for ARM6113 Technical Calculus: Compute the limits, derivatives, integrals and extrema of single-variable functions.

results.question: What canonical subject does alias code MID6113 map to?
results.answerPreview: Alias code MID6113 maps to MMD6123 Digital Fabrication and Prototyping.

results.question: What topics are covered in ARL6113 Electrical Circuits?
results.answerPreview: Course contents for ARL6113 Electrical Circuits: Circuit elements and signal waveforms; Basic laws of electric circuit; Network analysis; Sinusoidal steady state analysis; Transient analysis
